column2: MC
What is one of the reasons why the image named might not display?"

column3: The file might be located in a graphic folder so you would have to use src=graphic/JohnsPortrait.JPG""
The actual file name might be johnsPortrait.JPG (UPPER/lower case matter when listing file names)
The actual file name might be johnsPortrait.jpg (UPPER/lower case matter even on file extensions)
The image file is located somewhere else on the hard drive or on another flash drive and the browser is looking in the same folder as the web page.
The actual file name might be JohnsPortrait.png instead of .JPG.
All of these are reasons why an image might not display.

column2: Keep a checklist in your head: Do the file names match? Is the file in the correct folder? Is the filename spelled correctly?
MC
If an image file named chiefIngredientLogo.png is stored in a sub-folder named graphic, what is the correct way to display it on a web page?

column5: Spelling matters. Ingrediant should be spelled with an e" ingredient. Use CTRL C (copy) and CTRL V (paste) with filenames to make sure your code matches the actual filename."
Never start the path name with a slash /". That tells the browser to go all the way to the top of the directory tree (the root) to look for the file."
The logo is in the sub-folder named graphic so you have to include that as part of the file name so the browser knows where to find the image file.
Spelling matters. cheif should be spelled chief. Use CTRL C (copy) and CTRL V (paste) with filenames to make sure your code matches the actual filename.
Filenames matter. The file is a .png file but the browser is looking for a .jpg!
Spelling matters. Was the sub-folder named graphic or graphics?  Now you have to go into the file system and check...

column2: MC
Why won't this CSS code work?

column3: body is not a valid selector.
column5: body is a very valid selector. It is used to set defaults styles for the entire page such as font-family, font-size, and font-color.

column3: background: always requires multiple values such as url( ) and no-repeat.
column5: background: can have multiple values but it isn't required.

column3: The selector should be using parenthesis ( ) instead of curly braces { }
Each of the properties are missing a semi-colon ;" at the end."
column5: Those darn missing semi-colons will break all the lines that follow. Don't forget them!

column3: em is not a valid measurement for padding
column5: em is a very valid measurement and allows the padding to dynamically change as the size of the browser window changes.

column3: margin and padding must have at least four values for top, right, bottom, and left.
column5: margin and padding can have four values but it is not required. Two values designate top/bottom and left/right. One value uses that same measurement on all four sides of the object.
